sources.title: Christian Slater - The Good Shepherd (2004) The truth will test his faith to the limit.
sources.description:
Father Daniel Clemens (Christian Slater) uncovers a dangerous secret after a fellow priest is accused of a serious crime — now he's racing to expose a hidden conspiracy before it destroys him.  Teaming up with reporter Madeline Finney (Molly Parker), he dodges powerful enemies while unraveling a twisty mystery buried in the Church's deepest vaults.

Original title: The Good Shepherd (2004)
Alternative title: The Confessor

KEY CREDITS
Directed by: Lewin Webb
Screenplay by: Brad Mirman (The Vatican Tapes)
Based on the novel by: Daniel Easterman
Genres: Drama, Mystery, Thriller, Religon

STARRING
Christian Slater - Daniel Clemens
Molly Parker - Madeline Finney
Stephen Rea - McCaran
Gordon Pinsent - Cardinal Ledesna
Nancy Beatty - Lucy Gallagher
Von Flores - Father Andrews
Daniel Kash - Jeffrey Altman
Alex Paxton-Beesley - Monica

metadata.Plot: Laconic and self-contained, Edward Wilson (Matt Damon) heads C.I.A. covert operations during the Bay of Pigs. The agency suspects that Fidel Castro was tipped, so Wilson looks for the leak. As he investigates, he recalls, in a series of flashbacks, his father's death, student days at Yale (poetry; Skull and Bones), recruitment into the fledgling O.S.S., truncated affairs, a shotgun marriage, cutting his teeth on spy craft in London, distance from his son, the emergence of the Cold War, and relationships with agency, British, and Soviet counterparts. We watch his idealism give way to something else: disclosing the nature of that something else is at the heart of this movie's narration as he closes in on the leak.
metadata.Actors: Matt Damon, Angelina Jolie, Robert De Niro
metadata.Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 2 wins & 13 nominations total
